effect_description: Each A allele increases BMI by ~0.35 kg/m2 and obesity risk by ~1.2x
A allele (T on coding strand) confers lactase persistence in Europeans
C allele associated with lower desaturase activity and altered fatty acid levels
A allele (677T) reduces enzyme activity ~35% per allele; homozygous ~70% reduction
G allele (1298C) mildly reduces MTHFR activity; compound heterozygosity with C677T increases risk
AA homozygotes have higher BMI on high saturated fat diets
Part of PAV haplotype conferring strong bitter taste sensitivity
T allele (577X) causes alpha-actinin-3 deficiency; CC genotype favors sprint/power
T allele associated with reduced risk of cruciate ligament rupture
C allele associated with increased ligament and tendon injury risk
A allele (Gly482Ser) associated with reduced aerobic capacity
C allele associated with higher IL-6 levels and increased inflammation response
A allele associated with higher TNF-alpha production and inflammation
C allele = slow caffeine metabolizer; AC/CC genotypes at higher cardiovascular risk with high coffee intake
C allele (His48Arg) causes rapid alcohol to acetaldehyde conversion; protective against alcoholism
A allele causes alcohol flush reaction and acetaldehyde accumulation; very common in East Asians
T allele (Arg506Gln) causes activated protein C resistance; 3-8x increased VTE risk heterozygous
A allele (20210G>A) increases prothrombin levels; 2-3x increased VTE risk
Part of APOE e4 haplotype. e4 carriers have increased LDL and Alzheimer risk
Part of APOE e2 haplotype. e2/e2 associated with type III hyperlipoproteinemia
A allele (C282Y) most common HFE mutation; homozygous = high iron overload risk
G allele (H63D) mildly increases iron absorption; compound heterozygote C282Y/H63D increases risk
(TA)7 repeat (*28) reduces UGT1A1 expression; causes benign unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia
